ops.title: ccx vcs ship exits 1 \"no bookmark to advance\" when remote main moved under @ — the commit IS described, nothing pushed; recover with fetch/rebase/bookmark-move/push, never re-run ship
ops.body:
When `ccx vcs ship -m` fails with `ship: no bookmark to advance (heads(::@- & bookmarks()) matched none)`, remote `main` moved underneath the working copy (e.g. the guides bot pushed) so no bookmark is an ancestor of `@-`. At that point ship has ALREADY described `@` (the commit exists with the message) but pushed nothing. Re-running ship would describe the new empty `@` — don't.

**Recover:** `jj git fetch` → `jj rebase -s <change-id> -d main` (the empty `@` rides along; the bot's CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md churn merges in per [[cc-guides-render-regenerates-claude-md]]) → `jj bookmark move main --to <change-id>` → `jj git push` → watch CI yourself.

**Watching CI after a manual push:** resolve the FULL sha first — `git rev-parse <short-sha>` — then `gh run list --commit "$SHA"`. Never hand-pad a short sha into a fake full one: a wrong/short sha returns empty with exit 0 ([[gh-run-list-commit-full-sha]]) and a `-L1` fallback can silently watch someone else's run. Check every workflow on the commit (CI, Guides, cc-notes), not just the first.
